title: Validating Git Tag Names with Special Characters

statement.markdown: Write a function that validates a Git tag name. The tag name is valid if it matches the following pattern: `[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9._-]*`. However, the function should also handle edge cases where the input may be `null` or an empty string. The function should return `true` for valid tag names and `false` for invalid ones.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the Git tag name.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the tag name is valid.
statement.notes: The function should handle cases where the input is `null` or an empty string.

examples.explanation: Valid tag name with multiple dots
Invalid tag name with non-alphanumeric character

hints.text: Check for null or empty input strings
Use a regular expression to match the tag name pattern

editorial.title: Validating Git Tag Names
editorial.intuition: The key to solving this problem is to use a regular expression to match the tag name pattern.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible brute-force approach is to iterate over the input string and check each character individually.
editorial.betterSolution: A better approach is to use a regular expression to match the tag name pattern.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a regular expression to match the tag name pattern and check for null or empty input strings.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The regular expression `[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9._-]*` matches the required pattern.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to handle null or empty input strings
Not using a regular expression to match the tag name pattern
